front.title: Iterative Logic Refinement
front.subtitle: A recursive process where AI models critique and improve their own reasoning steps to reach higher accuracy.

back.text:
Iterative Logic Refinement is a computational technique used to enhance the problem-solving capabilities of large language models. Rather than generating a final answer in a single pass, the system treats its initial output as a draft. It then applies a series of self-correction loops, identifying logical inconsistencies or mathematical errors within its own chain of thought and revising them until a stable, verified solution is reached.

This method is a cornerstone of advanced reasoning models, allowing AI to tackle complex tasks in coding, mathematics, and symbolic logic. By mimicking the human habit of double-checking work, these systems can significantly reduce hallucinations and improve the reliability of their outputs. This shift from instant generation to deliberate, multi-step thinking marks a major evolution in artificial intelligence architecture.
